reasoning: The Federal Funds lower limit target rate is currently 3.50%. There are extremely low expectations of a rate hike by April 2026 (the FOMC meets late April with a 97.9% chance of holding). However, due to recent inflation fears, the futures market has started pricing in a 52% probability of a rate hike by the end of 2026. Therefore, probabilities of an increased rate (>3.50%) slowly scale up throughout 2026, reaching roughly 32% by October and 40% by mid-2027. Moving further into the future (2029-2036), probabilities are tempered by the historical base rate (which finds rates >3.50% roughly 35% of the time) and the instruction that uncertainty should rigorously increase with forecast horizons, cleanly converging towards 0.5 for the furthest dates.
